narrative.context: You have an interview in two hours. You feel nervous but energized. Your thoughts jump between hope and fear.
narrative.prompt: How do you use this emotional state to think more effectively?

options.label: Convert nervous energy into targeted rehearsal
options.description: Use the alertness to identify 3 likely questions and rehearse structured answers (STAR format) for 20 minutes.

ei.rationale: Uses emotion as information to focus attention and organize thinking—emotion facilitates preparation.

options.label: Do general reading to feel prepared
options.description: Skim many topics to reduce uncertainty, without a clear plan.

ei.rationale: May reduce anxiety somewhat, but the emotion is not used strategically to guide priority thinking.

options.label: Try to suppress the nervousness completely
options.description: Tell yourself emotions are weakness and push through without acknowledging them.

ei.rationale: Suppression often narrows attention; misses the signal value of emotion for planning.

options.label: Cancel preparation because it feels overwhelming
options.description: Avoid thinking about it until it’s time to leave.

ei.rationale: Avoidance prevents emotion from supporting thinking; reduces readiness.
